Name: Gambler
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Gambler
You have learned that fortune favors the prepared — and the reckless.
When luck could plausibly tip your way,
make a Luck Check. On a success, name one small true thing about this Scene. The GM builds from it.

Name: Field Medic
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Field Medic
You know what to do when someone is bleeding and the clock is running.
When precision matters and the pressure is high,
Choose one:
Steady the Hand — your hands do exactly what they must — take Boost 1 on the careful action
Hold Them Together — keep someone beside you calm enough to do their part
Work the Margin — narrate the small, exact thing that keeps it from getting worse

Name: Inspector
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Inspector
You notice what others walk past — the detail that changes everything.
While this card is in your hand, once per Scene study someone or something closely and ask one:
What here is not what it appears to be?
What is this person trying not to show?
What’s the fastest way this goes wrong?
The GM answers honestly.

Name: Trade Road Courier
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Trade Road Courier
You have carried messages and names across distances most people never cross.
When a familiar face could change how this moment goes,
declare who or what recognizes you and what you do. Make a Luck Check — the GM plays it accordingly.

Name: Cutpurse
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Cutpurse
Your hands know routes that eyes never track.
When precision and control matter more than force,
Choose one:
Leave No Trace — do it, and narrate why no one will know it was you
Light Fingers — take or place something small, unseen
Slip the Notice — move through a watched space as if you belong

Name: Pit Fighter
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Pit Fighter
You have stood in places that would break most people and walked out standing.
When you deliberately face something that would shake anyone else,
Choose one:
Walk Into It — close the distance you’re meant to flee — take Boost 1 on what it takes
Set the Tone — let them see you unbothered, and steady them by it
Stare It Down — make the threatening thing hesitate

Name: Musician
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Musician
You feel the rhythm of a room before anyone else names it.
Your internal clock never lies.
When timing is everything, choose one:
Right on the Beat — act at the exact moment it lands hardest
Set the Pace — give the group a rhythm to move on together
Feel the Turn — sense when the moment is about to change, and call it

Name: Street-Raised
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Street-Raised
The city taught you early that nothing stays still for long.
You’ve seen enough that very little surprises you anymore.
When things turn sharp or strange, choose one:
Already Knew — react as if you saw it coming, because part of you did
Read the Angle — spot who benefits, and say so
Keep Your Footing — stay steady, and give the others something to hold to

Name: Caravan Guard
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Caravan Guard
You have learned to read people faster than you read terrain.
When differences would be a barrier,
Choose one:
Find Common Ground — name the thing you both actually want
Speak Their Way — frame it so it lands for them, not you
Vouch — put your own standing behind someone to bridge the gap

Name: Pilgrim
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Pilgrim
You walk toward something larger than any single road.
When you meet someone driven by the same devotion,
Choose one:
Recognize the Road — name what you share; you both move easier for it
Offer the Path — give them a reason to walk alongside you
Invoke It — call on the shared cause to ask something of them

Name: Ascetic
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Ascetic
You have tested what you believe against pain, pressure, and doubt.
When your conviction is tested — by pain, pressure, or doubt —
Choose one:
Endure It — take the cost without bending, and let it show
Steady the Doubting — hold someone else’s faith up when theirs slips
Refuse the Bargain — turn down the easy way, and narrate why

Name: Pathfinder
HTML:
BackgroundAct
Pathfinder
You read uncertain ground the way others read faces.
When you’re navigating uncertain or dangerous ground,
Choose one:
Read the Land — name a feature of the terrain and how you’ll use it — take Boost 1 on the crossing
Find the Way Through — pick the route others would miss
Set the Trail — lead so the group moves safe and quick

Name: Warm Presence
HTML:
BondAct
Warm Presence
You don't have to do anything. You just have to be there.
Find
Someone carrying weight they have not named.
Act
Offer care or support without being asked.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Spark
HTML:
BondAct
The Spark
When things stall, you're the reason they start moving again.
Find
Someone who has disengaged or stalled.
Act
Draw them back in — a question, a nudge, a small invitation.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Steady Hands
HTML:
BondAct
Steady Hands
You've seen worse. More importantly, they know you've seen worse.
Find
Someone facing a moment that could shake them.
Act
Stay grounded and steady while the tension holds.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Listener
HTML:
BondAct
The Listener
You don't offer answers. You offer space.
Find
Someone with something they have not said aloud.
Act
Ask a genuine question and hold space for whatever comes back.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Sharp Eye
HTML:
BondAct
Sharp Eye
You notice what people need before they do.
Find
Someone who missed what you noticed.
Act
Share something specific you saw that they did not.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Confessor
HTML:
BondAct
The Confessor
People tell you things they haven't told anyone else.
Find
Someone who needs a witness, not advice.
Act
Make yourself fully open — no agenda, no judgment, just presence.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Loyal to a Fault
HTML:
BondAct
Loyal to a Fault
You've never needed a reason beyond: they're yours.
Find
Someone you would stand beside without needing a reason.
Act
Make clear, without qualification, that you are with them.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Levity
HTML:
BondAct
The Levity
You know when the room needs to breathe.
Find
A room that needs to breathe.
Act
Ease tension with something light — a joke, a story, a moment of warmth.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Quiet Respect
HTML:
BondAct
Quiet Respect
You don't say much. What you do say lands.
Find
Someone whose effort deserves to be named.
Act
Name something specific you noticed or respect about them.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Protector
HTML:
BondAct
The Protector
It's not about strength. It's about who you put yourself in front of.
Find
Someone who should not face what comes next alone.
Act
Commit, plainly, to facing it with them.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Honest Mirror
HTML:
BondAct
Honest Mirror
You tell people the truth because you respect them too much not to.
Find
Someone who needs truth more than comfort.
Act
Tell them something true and necessary, even when it is uncomfortable.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: The Witness
HTML:
BondAct
The Witness
You remember. You always remember.
Find
Someone whose moment deserves to be held.
Act
Tell them specifically what you have seen them do or endure.
Then
If it lands, you both gain Boost 1.

Name: Human
HTML:
AncestryAct
Human
You were not born with magic or ancient lineage. You were born with will.
When drive, adaptability, or sheer human determination tips the balance,
Choose one:
Press On — name what you're reaching toward — your ambition shapes what happens next
Find a Way — improvise, adapt, or simply refuse to be stumped; the GM confirms what opens up
Burn Bright — throw yourself in completely and succeed — the GM names what it costs you

Name: Elf
HTML:
AncestryAct
Elf
Something in you is older than this world, and it always notices.
passive
While this card is in your hand, once per Scene attune to your surroundings and ask one:
What here holds a history others have forgotten?
Is there magic at work in this place — obvious or not?
What should I know about this place that nobody thought to mention?
The GM answers honestly.

Name: Dwarf
HTML:
AncestryAct
Dwarf
You were carved from stone and stubbornness — and it shows.
When a blow — physical, social, or emotional — would stagger someone else,
Choose one:
Absorb It — take it and stay standing; you don't let the cost show
Shake It Off — make a Luck Check — on a success, your resilience turns the tide
Dig In — your refusal to be moved becomes a statement the Scene must acknowledge

Name: Halfling
HTML:
AncestryAct
Halfling
You've gotten away with things that should have ended you more times than you can count.
luck
Once per Scene, when you fail any check,
make a Luck Check. On a success, describe the absurd small thing that saves you — then reroll the failed check and take the new result.

Name: Half-Orc
HTML:
AncestryAct
Half-Orc
You have been underestimated your whole life. You have learned to use that.
When the Scene demands you give in, fall, or yield —
Choose one:
Relentless — stay standing on pure will; narrate how you refuse to go down
Show Your Fury — let your heritage surface; the room recalibrates around what it just saw
Survivor's Advantage — your familiarity with being hit gives you Boost 2 on your very next roll

Name: Tiefling
HTML:
AncestryAct
Tiefling
You carry something ancient and unholy in your blood. It is not a curse. It is yours.
When your fiendish heritage — the unease you inspire, the fire in your blood —
Choose one:
Cast the Shadow — your presence unsettles; name exactly what shifts in the Scene
Dark Gift — spend a Hit Die to gain Boost 2 on any one roll this Scene
Infernal Tongue — something in this Scene answers to what you carry; the GM confirms what opens

Name: Dragonborn
HTML:
AncestryAct
Dragonborn
Every choice you make is an answer to the question of what your bloodline means.
When the full weight of your draconic heritage — elemental, ancestral, primal —
Choose one:
Breath — unleash what runs in your veins; all in range must respond, evade, or endure
Dragon's Bearing — your stillness, your voice, your presence changes the room's temperature
Scaled — your natural resistance means a threat that stops others doesn't land the same on you

Name: Gnome
HTML:
AncestryAct
Gnome
Your mind moves in directions that simply do not occur to anyone else.
When cleverness, invention, or an angle nobody spotted changes what's possible,
Choose one:
Lateral Thinking — see the solution others missed; name it — the GM confirms it works
Tinker's Touch — use something in this Scene in a way its maker never intended
Gnomish Cunning — when magic or deception is aimed at you, gain Boost 2 to resist or see through it

Name: Half-Elf
HTML:
AncestryAct
Half-Elf
You belong to neither world completely. You have learned to make that a strength.
When being a bridge — between peoples, cultures, or ways of seeing — matters,
Choose one:
Common Ground — find the thread connecting two apparently opposed parties; name it and they both feel it
Read the Room — your experience across cultures means you know the rules here; act on it
Best of Both — name which heritage you're drawing on — elven grace or human drive — and show it

Name: Orc
HTML:
AncestryAct
Orc
You are built for things that require more than most people have.
When raw power, momentum, or the force of your presence is what the moment needs,
Choose one:
Rush — your momentum carries the Scene; name what breaks or gives way before you
Tower — plant yourself and refuse to move; your mass and will make you a wall the Scene works around
Battle Cry — your voice carries something older than words; all allies who hear it gain Boost on their next Action
